message.content:
You are an independent fix-confirmation reviewer (reviewer A) for a production-hardening pass on the hc-assay repo at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay. Be adversarial and skeptical — your job is to find any way the fix is wrong, incomplete, or its regression test fails to discriminate.

FINDING J-005 (MED):
Test discrimination gap: RateLimitError and PermanentReasoningError both SUBCLASS ReasoningError, so pytest.raises(ReasoningError) in the "maps to transient" tests does NOT actually discriminate a transient mapping from a rate-limit/permanent one — the test would pass even if T3 misclassified. Fix: _assert_transient(excinfo) asserts the raised exception is NOT an instance of (RateLimitError, PermanentReasoningError); applied to the empty-reply, generic-500, and mid-stream-raise transient tests. In tests/integration/test_reasoning_drain.py.

The complete code diff for all pass-7 fixes is at /tmp/pass7_code.diff (read it). The full files are under /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay. You may run the test suite (cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && source .venv/bin/activate && python -m pytest -q <path>) — NOTE: use "python -m pytest", not bare "pytest", or the tests/ package won't import.

Judge ONLY finding J-005. Decide:
- appropriate: does the fix address the real root cause with a sound approach (not a band-aid, not the wrong layer)?
- complete: is there any remaining gap, AND does the regression test actually FAIL if the fix is reverted (i.e. does it discriminate)? If you can, reason about or test revert-discrimination.
Return CONFIRMED only if both are true. Return CONCERN if appropriate but with a real residual gap. Return REJECTED if the fix is wrong or the test is non-discriminating. List concrete concerns.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll review finding J-005. Let me start by reading the diff and the relevant test file.

84	index 465013e..fb2ff89 100755
85	--- a/scripts/capture_transcripts.py
86	+++ b/scripts/capture_transcripts.py
87	@@ -73,7 +73,52 @@ def _username_pattern() -> re.Pattern[str] | None:
88	     return re.compile(re.escape(name))
89	 
90	 
91	+def _display_name_patterns() -> list[re.Pattern[str]]:
92	+    """Redactions for the operator's full DISPLAY NAME (#J-002).
93	+
94	+    The username/email rules leave the real name (e.g. a git ``user.name`` like "[REDACTED]")
95	+    in cleartext — distinct operator PII that appears in git author strings, ``pyproject``
96	+    ``authors=``, and prose, often right beside an already-redacted email. Derive candidate names
97	+    from ``git config user.name`` and the ``ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES`` env (comma-separated), redact each
98	+    (and, for a multi-word name, its whitespace-flexible form). Length-guarded to avoid
99	+    over-redacting common words.
100	+    """
101	+    names: list[str] = []
102	+    extra = os.environ.get("ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES", "")
103	+    names.extend(n.strip() for n in extra.split(",") if n.strip())
104	+    # Derive the operator name from SEVERAL sources — any one may be empty in a given context
105	+    # (#J-002 follow-up): on this node local `git config user.name` is unset yet commits still
106	+    # carry an author via global config / env, so a single-source lookup scrubbed nothing. Cover
107	+    # the env author vars, git config, AND the actual last-commit author.
108	+    for env_name in ("GIT_AUTHOR_NAME", "GIT_COMMITTER_NAME"):
109	+        v = os.environ.get(env_name, "").strip()
110	+        if v:
111	+            names.append(v)
112	+    try:
113	+        import subprocess
114	+
115	+        for cmd in (["git", "config", "user.name"], ["git", "log", "-1", "--format=%an"]):
116	+            out = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=5)
117	+            if out.returncode == 0 and out.stdout.strip():
118	+                names.append(out.stdout.strip())
119	+    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - git absent/misconfigured must never break capture
120	+        pass
121	+    pats: list[re.Pattern[str]] = []
122	+    seen: set[str] = set()
123	+    for n in names:
124	+        if len(n) < 4 or n in seen:
125	+            continue  # too short → risks over-redacting common words; or already added
126	+        seen.add(n)
127	+        # Flexible inter-token whitespace (e.g. "[REDACTED]"). NO leading \b: the name often
128	+        # appears glued to a JSON-escaped newline ("...---\\n[REDACTED]") where \b doesn't fire
129	+        # because the escape's 'n' abuts the name (#J-002 follow-up). A trailing \b still prevents
130	+        # matching inside a longer word (e.g. "Joneses"); the >=4-char guard bounds over-redaction.
131	+        pats.append(re.compile(r"\s+".join(re.escape(t) for t in n.split()) + r"\b"))
132	+    return pats
133	+
134	+
135	 _USERNAME_RE = _username_pattern()
136	+_DISPLAY_NAME_RES = _display_name_patterns()
137	 
138	 
139	 def _scrub(text: str) -> str:
140	@@ -88,6 +133,10 @@ def _scrub(text: str) -> str:
141	     # don't re-expose it. Done last so it also catches it inside project-dir slugs / git authors.
142	     if _USERNAME_RE is not None:
143	         text = _USERNAME_RE.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
144	+    # Redact the operator's full display name too (#J-002) — distinct PII the username/email rules
145	+    # leave in cleartext (git author strings, pyproject authors=, prose).
146	+    for pat in _DISPLAY_NAME_RES:
147	+        text = pat.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
148	     return text
149	 
150	 
151	@@ -104,13 +153,18 @@ def _copy_scrubbed(srcf: Path, dstf: Path) -> dict | None:
152	         # Scrub EVERY text file, not only .jsonl (#CV-O-1): the session subtree also holds .json
153	         # workflow/subagent transcripts that carry the same operator PII + credential-shaped
154	         # secrets — the old suffix==".jsonl" gate copied those RAW, bypassing redaction entirely.
155	-        # Decode strictly to classify: text → scrub; genuinely binary (rare here) → copy verbatim.
156	-        try:
157	-            text = raw.decode("utf-8")
158	-        except UnicodeDecodeError:
159	-            data = raw  # binary blob — nothing to scrub
160	+        #
161	+        # FAIL CLOSED on redaction (#J-001): classify binary by an actual binary SIGNAL (a NUL
162	+        # byte), NOT by UTF-8 validity. A transcript routinely embeds arbitrary subprocess stdout
163	+        # (latin-1 dumps, truncated multibyte) inside JSONL strings; gating on strict decode meant
164	+        # a single stray byte routed the WHOLE file to the verbatim branch and re-leaked it (the
165	+        # CV-O-1 fix had turned an always-redact path into a fail-open one). Decode with
166	+        # errors="replace" so redaction ALWAYS runs on text; only a genuinely binary blob (NUL
167	+        # present) is copied verbatim.
168	+        if b"\x00" in raw:
169	+            data = raw  # genuinely binary — nothing to scrub
170	         else:
171	-            data = _scrub(text).encode("utf-8")
172	+            data = _scrub(raw.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")).encode("utf-8")
173	         if dstf.exists() and dstf.read_bytes() == data:
174	             pass  # unchanged — still report it in the manifest
175	         else:

185	+        # check-then-create is a TOCTOU (#J-004): two concurrent first-time start_run calls (the
186	+        # 8-thread reasoning pool, or multiple study processes on one shared local store) can both
187	+        # see None and both create_experiment; MLflow rejects the loser with RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS
188	+        # and the exception would propagate out of start_run, leaving run_id=None and silently
189	+        # dropping ALL metrics + the provenance artifact for the whole study. On a create race,
190	+        # re-fetch the now-existing experiment instead of failing.
191	+        try:

193	+        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - normalize the concurrent-create race to a re-fetch

213	+    assert not isinstance(excinfo.value, (RateLimitError, PermanentReasoningError)), (

257	+def test_experiment_id_survives_concurrent_create_race(monkeypatch, tmp_path):
258	+    # #J-004: check-then-create is a TOCTOU — on the first-time race, create_experiment raises
259	+    # RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS for the loser. _experiment_id must re-fetch the now-existing
260	+    # experiment instead of propagating (which would leave run_id=None and drop all metrics).
261	+    monkeypatch.setenv("ASSAY_TRACKING_URI", f"sqlite:///{tmp_path / 'x.db'}")
262	+
263	+    class _Exp:
264	+        experiment_id = "exp-7"
265	+
266	+    class RacingClient:
267	+        def __init__(self):
268	+            self.calls = 0
269	+
270	+        def get_experiment_by_name(self, name):
271	+            # first call (the check) sees nothing; after the failed create, the winner's exists
272	+            self.calls += 1
273	+            return None if self.calls == 1 else _Exp()
274	+
275	+        def create_experiment(self, name):
276	+            raise RuntimeError("RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS: another writer created it")
277	+
278	+    t = MlflowExperimentTracker()
279	+    client = RacingClient()
280	+    assert t._experiment_id(client) == "exp-7"  # re-fetched, not raised
281	+
282	+
283	 def test_start_run_terminates_run_when_param_logging_fails(monkeypatch, tmp_path):
284	     # #F-023: if create_run succeeds but log_param fails, the run must be terminated FAILED, not
285	     # left orphaned in RUNNING state. Mock the MLflow client to exercise the failure path without
286	diff --git a/tests/test_scripts.py b/tests/test_scripts.py
287	index 510b00f..b66eceb 100644
288	--- a/tests/test_scripts.py
289	+++ b/tests/test_scripts.py
290	@@ -123,6 +123,39 @@ def test_copy_scrubbed_copies_binary_verbatim(tmp_path):
291	     assert dst.read_bytes() == b"\xff\xfe\x00\x01binary"
292	 
293	 
294	+def test_scrub_fails_closed_on_invalid_utf8_bytes():
295	+    # #J-001: a stray non-UTF-8 byte must NOT disable redaction for the whole file. The scrubber
296	+    # decodes errors='replace' and classifies binary by a NUL byte, so a secret next to an invalid
297	+    # byte is still scrubbed (the pass-6 strict-decode fix had made this fail OPEN).
298	+    cap = _load("scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "capture_transcripts_failclosed")
299	+    raw = b'{"out":"[REDACTED] caf\xe9 /Users/[REDACTED]/x"}'  # invalid \xe9
300	+    text = raw.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")
301	+    out = cap._scrub(text)
302	+    assert "[REDACTED]" not in out  # secret scrubbed despite the bad byte
303	+    assert "somebody" not in out

1	"""High-stakes (T3) stream-drain + error classification — exercised via synthetic SDK messages.
2	
3	The real `_high_stakes_complete._drain` and its 429/auth/error/empty classification is the most
4	dangerous untested code (it adjudicates high-stakes verdicts and maps subscription rate
5	windows). Validating it against the real frontier costs a metered call and only runs when
6	`ASSAY_RUN_HIGH_STAKES_TESTS=1`. Here we drive the REAL drain logic by injecting a fake
7	`claude_agent_sdk` whose `query` yields synthetic `AssistantMessage`/`RateLimitEvent`/
8	`ResultMessage` objects — covering the classification branches in CI with no metered call.
9	
10	Requires anyio (the reasoning extra is async); skips otherwise.
11	"""
12	
13	from __future__ import annotations
14	
15	import sys
16	import types
17	
18	import pytest
19	
20	pytest.importorskip("anyio")
21	
22	from assay_engine.reasoning import seam as rc  # noqa: E402
23	from assay_engine.reasoning.seam import (  # noqa: E402
24	    PermanentReasoningError,
25	    RateLimitError,
26	    ReasoningError,
27	)
28	
29	
30	class _TextBlock:
31	    def __init__(self, text):
32	        self.text = text
33	
34	
35	class _AssistantMessage:
36	    def __init__(self, content):
37	        self.content = content
38	
39	
40	class _ResultMessage:
41	    def __init__(self, is_error=False, api_error_status=None, errors=None, stop_reason=None):
42	        self.is_error = is_error
43	        self.api_error_status = api_error_status
44	        self.errors = errors
45	        self.stop_reason = stop_reason
46	
47	
48	class RateLimitEvent:  # name matters: the engine matches by type(msg).__name__ (real SDK contract)
49	    def __init__(self, status):
50	        self.rate_limit_info = types.SimpleNamespace(status=status)
51	
52	
53	class _ClaudeAgentOptions:
54	    def __init__(self, **kw):
55	        self.__dict__.update(kw)
56	
57	
58	_HANG = object()  # sentinel: query awaits "forever" to simulate a hung subprocess (#127)
59	
60	
61\[REDACTED]
62	def fake_sdk(monkeypatch):
63	    """Inject a fake claude_agent_sdk whose `query` yields a scripted message stream.
64	
65	    Exposes the captured ClaudeAgentOptions via ``scripted.captured_options`` so tests can assert
66	    the security wiring (env=scrubbed_env, sandbox flags) actually reaches the subprocess.
67	    """
68	    import anyio
69	
70	    class _Scripted(list):
71	        captured_options = None  # query records the ClaudeAgentOptions it received here
72	
73	    scripted = _Scripted()
74	
75	    async def query(prompt, options):  # noqa: ANN001
76	        scripted.captured_options = options
77	        for msg in scripted:
78	            if msg is _HANG:  # simulate a hung subprocess; inner fail_after must cancel us
79	                await anyio.sleep(3600)
80	            if isinstance(msg, Exception):  # simulate the SDK/transport raising mid-stream
81	                raise msg
82	            yield msg
83	
84	    mod = types.ModuleType("claude_agent_sdk")
85	    mod.AssistantMessage = _AssistantMessage
86	    mod.ResultMessage = _ResultMessage
87	    mod.TextBlock = _TextBlock
88	    mod.ClaudeAgentOptions = _ClaudeAgentOptions
89	    mod.query = query
90	    monkeypatch.setitem(sys.modules, "claude_agent_sdk", mod)
91	    monkeypatch.setenv("CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN", "test-subscription-token")
92	    return scripted
93	
94	
95	def _run(prompt="hi", system=None, model=None):
96	    return rc._high_stakes_complete(prompt, system, model)
97	
98	
99	def test_t3_success_collects_text_blocks(fake_sdk):
100	    fake_sdk.append(_AssistantMessage([_TextBlock("po"), _TextBlock("ng")]))
101	    assert _run() == "pong"
102	
103	
104	def test_t3_wires_scrubbed_env_into_subprocess_options(fake_sdk, monkeypatch):
105	    # #124: the no-metered-API firewall must be wired end-to-end — env=scrubbed_env() must reach
106	    # the ClaudeAgentOptions handed to the subprocess (not just be unit-tested in isolation).
107	    monkeypatch.setenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY", "[REDACTED]")
108	    monkeypatch.setenv("ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL", "http://attacker.example")
109	    fake_sdk.append(_AssistantMessage([_TextBlock("pong")]))
110	    _run()
111	    opts = fake_sdk.captured_options
112	    assert hasattr(opts, "env"), "env not wired into ClaudeAgentOptions (#124 firewall unguarded)"
113	    # the merged child env must neutralize the metered key + off-box redirect, keep OAuth
114	    child = {**__import__("os").environ, **opts.env}
115	    assert child["ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"] == "" and child["ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL"] == ""
116	    assert child["CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN"] == "test-subscription-token"
117	
118	
119	def test_t3_wires_subprocess_sandbox_settings(fake_sdk):
120	    # #125: the subprocess sandbox flags must actually reach the options (no tools, no settings
121	    # sources, non-interactive permission mode).
122	    fake_sdk.append(_AssistantMessage([_TextBlock("pong")]))
123	    _run()
124	    opts = fake_sdk.captured_options
125	    assert opts.allowed_tools == []  # no tools may run in a reasoning call
126	    assert opts.setting_sources == []  # do not load ambient project/user settings
127	    assert opts.permission_mode == "dontAsk"  # non-interactive, deny-by-default
128	
129	
130	def test_t3_hung_subprocess_is_cancelled_not_leaked(fake_sdk, monkeypatch):
131	    # #127: a hung stream must be cancelled by the inner timeout (frees the pool slot) and
132	    # surface a typed ReasoningError — not block forever.
133	    monkeypatch.setattr(rc, "HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT", 0.3)
134	    fake_sdk.append(_HANG)
135	    import time as _t
136	
137	    t0 = _t.monotonic()
138	    with pytest.raises(ReasoningError):
139	        _run()
140	    assert _t.monotonic() - t0 < 5  # cancelled promptly near the 0.3s inner timeout, not hung
141	
142	
143	def _assert_transient(excinfo):
144	    # #J-005: RateLimitError and PermanentReasoningError both SUBCLASS ReasoningError, so
145	    # `pytest.raises(ReasoningError)` does NOT discriminate a transient mapping from a rate-limit
146	    # or permanent one. Assert the exception is the transient class itself, not a decisive subclass.
147	    assert not isinstance(excinfo.value, (RateLimitError, PermanentReasoningError)), (
148	        f"expected a transient ReasoningError, got {type(excinfo.value).__name__}"
149	    )
150	
151	
152	def test_t3_empty_reply_raises_transient(fake_sdk):
153	    fake_sdk.append(_AssistantMessage([]))  # no text blocks -> empty
154	    with pytest.raises(ReasoningError) as ei:
155	        _run()
156	    _assert_transient(ei)
157	
158	
159	def test_t3_rate_limit_event_rejected_maps_to_ratelimit(fake_sdk):
160	    fake_sdk.append(RateLimitEvent("rejected"))
161	    with pytest.raises(RateLimitError):
162	        _run()
163	
164	
165	def test_t3_rate_limit_allowed_is_not_an_error(fake_sdk):
166	    # an informational 'allowed' rate event must NOT trigger backpressure; text still returns
167	    fake_sdk.append(RateLimitEvent("allowed"))
168	    fake_sdk.append(_AssistantMessage([_TextBlock("ok")]))
169	    assert _run() == "ok"
170	
171	
172	def test_t3_auth_error_maps_to_permanent(fake_sdk):
173	    fake_sdk.append(_ResultMessage(is_error=True, api_error_status=401, errors=["unauth"]))
174	    with pytest.raises(PermanentReasoningError):
175	        _run()
176	
177	
178	def test_t3_generic_error_maps_to_transient(fake_sdk):
179	    fake_sdk.append(_ResultMessage(is_error=True, api_error_status=500, errors=["boom"]))
180	    with pytest.raises(ReasoningError) as ei:
181	        _run()
182	    _assert_transient(ei)  # a 500 must be transient, NOT permanent/rate-limit (#J-005)
183	
184	
185	def test_t3_429_not_clobbered_by_trailing_result_error(fake_sdk):
186	    # a RateLimitEvent(rejected) must win over a later ResultMessage error (preserve backpressure)
187	    fake_sdk.append(RateLimitEvent("rejected"))
188	    fake_sdk.append(_ResultMessage(is_error=True, api_error_status=500))
189	    with pytest.raises(RateLimitError):
190	        _run()
191	
192	
193	def test_t3_overloaded_529_maps_to_ratelimit(fake_sdk):
194	    fake_sdk.append(_ResultMessage(is_error=True, api_error_status=529, errors=["overloaded"]))
195	    with pytest.raises(RateLimitError):
196	        _run()
197	
198	
199	def test_t3_forbidden_403_maps_to_permanent(fake_sdk):
200	    fake_sdk.append(_ResultMessage(is_error=True, api_error_status=403, errors=["forbidden"]))
201	    with pytest.raises(PermanentReasoningError):
202	        _run()
203	
204	
205	def test_t3_top_level_rejected_status_maps_to_ratelimit(fake_sdk):
206	    # a non-RateLimitEvent message carrying status=='rejected' is the second 429 path
207	    msg = types.SimpleNamespace(status="rejected")
208	    fake_sdk.append(msg)
209	    with pytest.raises(RateLimitError):
210	        _run()
211	
212	
213	def test_t3_sdk_raises_mid_stream_maps_to_transient(fake_sdk):
214	    # the SDK/transport raising (rather than completing the stream) is a transient failure
215	    fake_sdk.append(RuntimeError("connection reset"))
216	    with pytest.raises(ReasoningError) as ei:
217	        _run()
218	    _assert_transient(ei)  # transport raise must be transient, not permanent/rate-limit (#J-005)
219	
220	
221	def test_t3_missing_auth_fails_loud(monkeypatch, fake_sdk):
222	    monkeypatch.delenv("CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN", raising=False)
223	    monkeypatch.setattr(rc, "_high_stakes_auth_present", lambda: False)
224	    with pytest.raises(PermanentReasoningError, match="subscription auth"):
225	        _run()
226

content.text:
Key observation: there is no `TransientReasoningError` class. "Transient" is represented by raising the **base** `ReasoningError` directly (lines 489, 498, 502 etc.). So the retry logic at lines 599-608 treats base-class `ReasoningError` as retriable, `PermanentReasoningError` and `RateLimitError` as special. The fix's approach — asserting NOT an instance of the two decisive subclasses — is exactly the correct discriminator.

Let me verify the empty-reply, generic-500, and mid-stream paths and confirm revert-discrimination by running the tests, plus testing what happens if I simulate a misclassification.

input.command: cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && source .venv/bin/activate && python -m pytest -q tests/integration/test_reasoning_drain.py 2>&1 | tail -15

content.text: All pass. Now let me prove the helper actually discriminates — verify that `_assert_transient` would FAIL if the raised exception were a `RateLimitError` or `PermanentReasoningError`. I'll directly test the helper's logic with a mock excinfo.

input.command:
cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && source .venv/bin/activate && python -c "
import types
from assay_engine.reasoning.seam import ReasoningError, RateLimitError, PermanentReasoningError
import tests.integration.test_reasoning_drain as t

def check(exc):
    ei = types.SimpleNamespace(value=exc)
    try:
        t._assert_transient(ei)
        return 'PASS'
    except AssertionError as e:
        return 'FAIL: ' + str(e)

print('base ReasoningError ->', check(ReasoningError('x')))
print('RateLimitError      ->', check(RateLimitError('x')))
print('PermanentReasoningError ->', check(PermanentReasoningError('x')))
"
input.description: Verify discriminator distinguishes all three classes

content.content:
base ReasoningError -> PASS
RateLimitError      -> FAIL: expected a transient ReasoningError, got RateLimitError
PermanentReasoningError -> FAIL: expected a transient ReasoningError, got PermanentReasoningError

content.text:
The discriminator is sound. Now let me confirm revert-discrimination concretely: if the fix were reverted (i.e. just `pytest.raises(ReasoningError)` with no `_assert_transient`), the test would pass even on misclassification. Let me verify the three transient paths genuinely raise the base class (not a subclass) so the assertion is meaningful, and check there's no path where a legitimately transient case raises something the helper would wrongly reject.

The three covered tests:
- `test_t3_empty_reply_raises_transient` → line 502 `raise ReasoningError("...empty reply")` — base class. Correct.
- `test_t3_generic_error_maps_to_transient` (500) → line 498 `raise ReasoningError("...status=...")` — base class. Correct.
- `test_t3_sdk_raises_mid_stream` → line 489 `raise ReasoningError("...call failed")` — base class. Correct.

Let me confirm the revert-discrimination by simulating a misclassification at the source to be fully adversarial.

input.command:
cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && source .venv/bin/activate && python -c "
# Simulate revert (old test) vs new test under a MISCLASSIFICATION.
# Old test: pytest.raises(ReasoningError) only. New: + _assert_transient.
from assay_engine.reasoning.seam import ReasoningError, RateLimitError, PermanentReasoningError
import pytest, types
import tests.integration.test_reasoning_drain as t

# Pretend the engine misclassified an empty reply as Permanent (a real T3 bug).
exc = PermanentReasoningError('misclassified empty reply')

# OLD test behavior:
old_pass = isinstance(exc, ReasoningError)  # pytest.raises(ReasoningError) would catch it
print('OLD test (reverted) would PASS on misclassification:', old_pass)

# NEW test behavior:
ei = types.SimpleNamespace(value=exc)
try:
    t._assert_transient(ei); new_pass=True
except AssertionError: new_pass=False
print('NEW test PASSES on misclassification:', new_pass)
"

content.content:
OLD test (reverted) would PASS on misclassification: True
NEW test PASSES on misclassification: False

content.text:
Conclusively demonstrated. The reverted (old) test would silently pass even if T3 misclassified a transient failure as permanent/rate-limit; the new test fails. The fix genuinely closes the discrimination gap.

Let me do one final adversarial check: are there other "transient" tests in the file that should have gotten the same treatment but didn't? The finding scopes to three tests. Let me confirm no other test claims "transient" without the guard, and verify the decisive-subclass tests (rate-limit/permanent) are themselves discriminating.

The remaining mapping tests use `pytest.raises(RateLimitError)` / `pytest.raises(PermanentReasoningError)` directly — those ARE discriminating already (a base `ReasoningError` would not satisfy `pytest.raises` of a subclass). So they need no fix. The only non-discriminating cases were the three transient ones, all now fixed. No other test in the file claims transient mapping.

Coverage is complete and correctly scoped. The approach targets the root cause (the test's inability to distinguish base from subclass), at the right layer (the test assertion), and is not a band-aid.

input.rationale: Verified the premise: in src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py both PermanentReasoningError (line 70) and RateLimitError (line 74) subclass ReasoningError (line 66), and "transient" is encoded as raising the BASE ReasoningError directly (lines 489/498/502). So the old `pytest.raises(ReasoningError)` could not distinguish a transient mapping from a permanent/rate-limit one — the documented gap is real. The fix's `_assert_transient` asserts the raised exception is NOT an instance of (RateLimitError, PermanentReasoningError), which is the exact correct discriminator at the correct (test) layer; not a band-aid. Confirmed discrimination empirically: the helper PASSES on base ReasoningError and FAILS on both RateLimitError and PermanentReasoningError. Confirmed revert-discrimination: simulating a T3 misclassification (empty reply raised as PermanentReasoningError), the reverted/old test PASSES (false negative) while the new test FAILS — so the regression test genuinely discriminates. Scope is correct: only the three transient tests (empty-reply, generic-500, mid-stream-raise) raise the base class and needed the guard; the rate-limit/permanent tests already discriminate because pytest.raises of a subclass excludes the base. Full file (15 tests) passes. No other transient-claiming test lacks the guard.

content.text: Finding J-005 reviewed: CONFIRMED. The fix correctly closes the test-discrimination gap, and the regression test demonstrably fails on a reverted/misclassified scenario.
